select_when: Ownership, authorship, or incumbency may be making an asset, idea, or status quo feel more valuable than it would to an outsider.
A negotiation, divestment, or priority decision is being warped by identity, history, or control rather than objective utility alone.
The team needs to test whether attachment to what it already has is hiding opportunity cost or suppressing rational switching.
The next move should force an outside-view valuation instead of letting possession decide the price.
avoid_when: The issue is already being evaluated through a credible outside-view or neutral benchmark and additional ownership-bias framing would add little.
The model is being used as a loose story about attachment without any real effort to compare today's best available alternative.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on overvaluing what is already owned, outside-view valuation, and anti-inertia discipline in negotiation and prioritization.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader behavioral-economics sprawl because the raw source is strongest on ownership distortion, divestment failure, and valuation reset.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans role reversal, negotiation posture, and written-plan discipline.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on ownership distorting value, resource prioritization, and status-quo attachment warping choice quality.

avoid_when.note: The source is stronger on activation and anti-patterns than on explicit anti-activation bullets, so these avoid-when conditions are conservative reviewed compressions.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on ownership-distorted valuation, while the typed input remains a normalized operational label.

output_type.note: The source supports outside-view valuation and detachment moves; the output label compresses that into a reusable intervention artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Endowment-effect is strongest as diagnostic and metacognitive correction of ownership-driven judgment.
